message.content:
Rewrite the file `docs/api.md` to match the current JavaFX 3.0.0-alpha.1 state.

Read these files first:
- `/Users/phoebej/Develop/Java/SwissKitJ/SwissKitJ-Api/src/main/java/fan/summer/api/SwissKitJPlugin.java`
- `/Users/phoebej/Develop/Java/SwissKitJ/SwissKitJ-Api/src/main/java/fan/summer/api/StepWizard.java` (or search for it with Glob `**/StepWizard.java`)
- `/Users/phoebej/Develop/Java/SwissKitJ/SwissKitJ-Api/src/main/java/fan/summer/api/log/LoggerFactory.java` (or search for it)
- `/Users/phoebej/Develop/Java/SwissKitJ/CLAUDE.md` ("Plugin Development" and "Plugin logging" sections)
- Search for enum files matching `Category.java`, `Type.java`, `IconStyle.java` in SwissKitJ-Api

Key API changes:
- `SwissKitJPlugin` replaces `KitPage` (Swing era)
- No more `@SwissKitPage` annotation
- Enums: `Category` (TEXT/IMAGE/DEV/NET/OTHER), `Type` (BUILTIN/PLUGIN), `IconStyle` (IC_BLUE/IC_PURPLE/IC_TEAL/IC_AMBER/IC_RED/IC_PINK/IC_GRAY)
- `StepWizard` is the reusable wizard component
- `LoggerFactory` + `PluginLogger` for plugin logging
- `Themes.applyTo(Scene)` for applying common CSS to separate Stages

Remove all references to:
- `KitPage` interface
- `@SwissKitPage` annotation
- `GradientProgressBar`, `FixedWidthComboBox`, `SideMenuBar`, `TableUtil` (deleted Swing components)
- SwingWorker, JPanel, JTable, SwingUtilities

Write the file completely. Document the current API properly with code examples for the SwissKitJPlugin interface, enums, StepWizard, and LoggerFactory.

attachment.content:
- docs-updater: Docs Updater Agent
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1 # API Reference 2 3 This section provides detailed API documentation for SwissKit's core interfaces and classes. 4 5 ## Table of Contents 6 7 - [Module Overview](#module-overview) 8 - [KitPage Interface](#kitpage-interface) 9 - [@SwissKitPage Annotation](#swisskitpage-annotation) 10 - [SwissKitPageScanner](#swisskitpagescanner) 11 - [Database Layer](#database-layer) 12 - [UI Components](#plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui-components) 13 - [Worker Classes](#plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker-classes) 14 - [Utility Classes](#utility-classes) 15 - [Listeners](#listeners) 16 - [Entity Classes](#entity-classes) 17 18 ## Module Overview 19 20 SwissKit uses a multi-module architecture. Key components are organized as follows: 21 22 | Module | Package | Description | 23 |-------------------|------------------------------------------------|----------------------------| 24 | `SwissKitJ-Api` | `fan.summer.api` | Core interfaces (KitPage) | 25 | `SwissKitJ-Api` | `fan.summer.annoattion` | Annotations (SwissKitPage) | 26 | `SwissKitJ-Api` | `fan.summer.plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui.components` | Shared UI components | 27 | `SwissKit` (main) | `fan.summer.database` | Database layer | 28 | `SwissKit` (main) | `fan.summer.kitpage` | Tool implementations | 29 | `SwissKit` (main) | `fan.summer.utils` | Utility classes | 30 31 --- 32 33 ## KitPage Interface 34 35 The `KitPage` interface is the foundation for all tool pages in SwissKit. It is located in the `SwissKitJ-Api` module. 36 37 ### Location 38 39 ``` 40 SwissKitJ-Api/src/main/java/fan/summer/api/KitPage.java 41 ``` 42 43 ### Interface Definition 44 45 ```java 46 package fan.summer.api; 47 48 import fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage; 49 50 import javax.swing.*; 51 52 /** 53 * Interface for SwissKit plugin pages. 54 * All plugin pages must implement this interface and be annotated with {@link SwissKitPage}. 55 */ 56 public interface KitPage { 57 /** 58 * Returns the main panel for this page. 59 * This panel will be displayed in the content area when the page is selected. 60 * 61 * @return the JPanel containing the page content 62 */ 63 JPanel getPanel(); 64 65 /** 66 * Returns the menu display name. 67 * Default implementation reads from {@link SwissKitPage#menuName()} annotation. 68 * 69 * @return menu display name 70 */ 71 default String getMenuName() { 72 SwissKitPage annotation = getClass().getAnnotation(SwissKitPage.class); 73 if (annotation != null && !annotation.menuName().isEmpty()) { 74 return annotation.menuName(); 75 } 76 return getClass().getSimpleName(); 77 } 78 79 /** 80 * Returns the menu icon. 81 * Override this method to provide a custom icon for the menu item. 82 * 83 * @return menu icon, or null for no icon 84 */ 85 default Icon getMenuIcon() { 86 return null; 87 } 88 89 /** 90 * Returns the menu tooltip text shown on hover. 91 * Default implementation reads from {@link SwissKitPage#menuTooltip()} annotation. 92 * 93 * @return tooltip text, or null for no tooltip 94 */ 95 default String getMenuTooltip() { 96 SwissKitPage annotation = getClass().getAnnotation(SwissKitPage.class); 97 if (annotation != null) { 98 return annotation.menuTooltip(); 99 } 100 return null; 101 } 102 } 103 ``` 104 105 ### Methods 106 107 | Method | Return Type | Description | 108 |--------------------|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------| 109 | `getPanel()` | `JPanel` | Returns the main panel containing all UI elements | 110 | `getMenuName()` | `String` | Returns the menu display name (default: from annotation) | 111 | `getMenuIcon()` | `Icon` | Returns the menu icon (default: `null`) | 112 | `getMenuTooltip()` | `String` | Returns the menu tooltip (default: from annotation) | 113 114 ### Example Implementation 115 116 ```java 117 import fan.summer.api.KitPage; 118 import fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage; 119 120 @SwissKitPage( 121 menuName = "📋 Example", 122 menuTooltip = "Open Example Tool", 123 visible = true, 124 order = 10 125 ) 126 public class ExamplePage implements KitPage { 127 private JPanel panel; 128 129 public ExamplePage() { 130 panel = new JPanel(); 131 panel.add(new JLabel("Example Page")); 132 } 133 134 @Override 135 public JPanel getPanel() { 136 return panel; 137 } 138 } 139 ``` 140 141 --- 142 143 ## @SwissKitPage Annotation 144 145 The `@SwissKitPage` annotation configures tool page menu properties. It is located in the `SwissKitJ-Api` module. 146 147 ### Location 148 149 ``` 150 SwissKitJ-Api/src/main/java/fan/summer/annoattion/SwissKitPage.java 151 ``` 152 153 ### Annotation Definition 154 155 ```java 156 package fan.summer.annoattion; 157 158 import java.lang.annotation.*; 159 160 /** 161 * Annotation to mark a class as a KitPage implementation. 162 * Used for automatic page registration and menu configuration. 163 */ 164 @Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME) 165 @Target(ElementType.TYPE) 166 public @interface SwissKitPage { 167 /** 168 * Menu display name shown in the sidebar. 169 */ 170 String menuName() default ""; 171 172 /** 173 * Menu tooltip text shown on hover. 174 */ 175 String menuTooltip() default ""; 176 177 /** 178 * Whether this page is visible in the menu. 179 */ 180 boolean visible() default true; 181 182 /** 183 * Menu item order for sorting. 184 * Lower values appear first in the menu. 185 */ 186 int order() default 0; 187 } 188 ``` 189 190 ### Properties 191 192 | Property | Type | Default | Description | 193 |---------------|---------|---------|-------------------------------| 194 | `menuName` | String | "" | Display name in sidebar | 195 | `menuTooltip` | String | "" | Tooltip on hover | 196 | `visible` | boolean | true | Whether to show in menu | 197 | `order` | int | 0 | Display order (lower = first) | 198 199 --- 200 201 ## SwissKitPageScanner 202 203 The `SwissKitPageScaner` class automatically discovers and loads all `KitPage` implementations. 204 205 ### Location 206 207 ``` 208 src/main/java/fan/summer/scaner/SwissKitPageScaner.java 209 ``` 210 211 ```java 212 package fan.summer.scaner; 213 214 public class SwissKitPageScaner { 215 /** 216 * Scans for KitPage implementations using SPI ServiceLoader. 217 * 218 * @return list of visible KitPage instances sorted by order 219 */ 220 public static List scan() { 221 // Implementation 222 } 223 } 224 ``` 225 226 ### Key Features 227 228 - **SPI-based**: Uses Java ServiceLoader mechanism 229 - **Visibility Filter**: Only includes pages with `visible = true` 230 - **Order Sorting**: Sorts by `order()` value in annotation 231 - **Auto-Instantiation**: Creates page instances automatically 232 233 --- 234 235 ## Database Layer 236 237 ### DatabaseInit 238 239 Handles database initialization and MyBatis configuration. 240 241 ```java 242 package fan.summer.database; 243 244 public class DatabaseInit { 245 private static SqlSessionFactory sqlSessionFactory; 246 247 /** 248 * Initializes database and MyBatis. 249 * Creates tables from init.sql if not exist. 250 */ 251 public static void init() { } 252 253 /** 254 * Returns a new SqlSession for database operations. 255 */ 256 public static SqlSession getSqlSession() { } 257 258 /** 259 * Returns the SqlSessionFactory. 260 */ 261 public static SqlSessionFactory getSqlSessionFactory() { } 262 } 263 ``` 264 265 ### Entity Classes 266 267 #### EmailAddressBookEntity 268 269 ```java 270 @Data 271 public class EmailAddressBookEntity { 272 private Integer id; 273 private String emailAddress; 274 private String nickname; 275 private String tags; // JSON array 276 } 277 ``` 278 279 #### EmailTagEntity 280 281 ```java 282 @Data 283 public class EmailTagEntity { 284 private Long id; 285 private String tag; 286 } 287 ``` 288 289 #### EmailSentLogEntity 290 291 ```java 292 @Data 293 public class EmailSentLogEntity { 294 private Long id; 295 private String to; 296 private String cc; 297 private String bcc; 298 private String subject; 299 private String content; 300 private String attachment; 301 private Date sendTime; 302 private boolean isSuccess; 303 } 304 ``` 305 306 #### ComplexSplitConfigEntity 307 308 ```java 309 @Data 310 public class ComplexSplitConfigEntity { 311 private Long id; 312 private String taskId; 313 private String fieldName; 314 private String sheetName; 315 private Integer headerIndex; 316 private Integer columnIndex; 317 } 318 ``` 319 320 ### Mapper Interfaces 321 322 #### EmailAddressBookMapper 323 324 ```java 325 public interface EmailAddressBookMapper { 326 void insert(EmailAddressBookEntity entity); 327 List selectEmailAddressBook(); 328 } 329 ``` 330 331 #### EmailTagMapper 332 333 ```java 334 public interface EmailTagMapper { 335 void insert(EmailTagEntity entity); 336 void update(EmailTagEntity entity); 337 List selectAll(); 338 } 339 ``` 340 341 #### EmailSentLogMapper 342 343 ```java 344 public interface EmailSentLogMapper { 345 void insert(EmailSentLogEntity entity); 346 List selectAll(); 347 } 348 ``` 349 350 --- 351 352 ## UI Components 353 354 UI components are located in the `SwissKitJ-Api` module for shared use across the main application and plugins. 355 356 ### GradientProgressBar 357 358 A progress bar with gradient colors and smooth animation. 359 360 **Location**: `SwissKitJ-Api/src/main/java/fan/summer/plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/components/GradientProgressBar.java` 361 362 #### Constructor 363 364 ```java 365 public GradientProgressBar() 366 ``` 367 368 #### Methods 369 370 | Method | Return Type | Description | 371 |-------------------------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------| 372 | `setValue(int value)` | `void` | Sets the current value (triggers animation) | 373 | `setStringPainted(boolean painted)` | `void` | Enables/disables text display | 374 | `setString(String text)` | `void` | Sets the text to display | 375 376 #### Example Usage 377 378 ```java 379 GradientProgressBar progressBar = new GradientProgressBar(); 380 progressBar.setMinimum(0); 381 progressBar.setMaximum(100); 382 progressBar.setStringPainted(true); 383 progressBar.setValue(50); 384 progressBar.setString("Processing... 50%"); 385 ``` 386 387 ### FixedWidthComboBox 388 389 A combo box with fixed width. 390 391 **Location**: `SwissKitJ-Api/src/main/java/fan/summer/plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/components/FixedWidthComboBox.java` 392 393 #### Constructor 394 395 ```java 396 public FixedWidthComboBox(int width) 397 ``` 398 399 ### SideMenuBar 400 401 Dynamic side menu component. 402 403 **Location**: `src/main/java/fan/summer/plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/sidebar/SideMenuBar.java` 404 405 #### Constructor 406 407 ```java 408 public SideMenuBar(List pages, JPanel contentPanel) 409 ``` 410 411 #### Methods 412 413 | Method | Return Type | Description | 414 |------------------------------------|-------------|-------------------------------| 415 | `addPage(KitPage page)` | `void` | Adds a page to the menu | 416 | `removePage(int index)` | `void` | Removes a page from the menu | 417 | `selectPage(int index)` | `void` | Sets the selected page | 418 | `rebuildMenu()` | `void` | Rebuilds menu from pages list | 419 | `setPages(List newPages)` | `void` | Sets new page list | 420 421 ### TableUtil 422 423 Utility class for consistent JTable initialization. 424 425 **Location**: `src/main/java/fan/summer/utils/plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/TableUtil.java` 426 427 ```java 428 public abstract class TableUtil { 429 /** 430 * Initializes a JTable with specified columns and data. 431 * 432 * @param table the JTable to initialize 433 * @param columns column names 434 * @param rowData list of row data arrays 435 * @param isCellEditableIndex columns after this index are editable 436 * @return the initialized JTable 437 */ 438 public static JTable initTable(JTable table, String[] columns, 439 List rowData, int isCellEditableIndex) { 440 DefaultTableModel model = new DefaultTableModel(columns, 0) { 441 @Override 442 public boolean isCellEditable(int row, int column) { 443 return column > isCellEditableIndex; 444 } 445 }; 446 for (Object[] row : rowData) { 447 model.addRow(row); 448 } 449 table.setModel(model); 450 return table; 451 } 452 } 453 ``` 454 455 --- 456 457 ## Worker Classes 458 459 ### QueryAllEmailInfoWorker 460 461 Background plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker for loading email contacts. 462 463 #### Constructor 464 465 ```java 466 public QueryAllEmailInfoWorker(QueryAllEmailInfoCallBack callBack) 467 ``` 468 469 #### Example Usage 470 471 ```java 472 new QueryAllEmailInfoWorker(new QueryAllEmailInfoCallBack() { 473 @Override 474 public void onSuccess(List result) { 475 // Handle success 476 } 477 478 @Override 479 public void onFailure(Exception e) { 480 // Handle error 481 } 482 }).execute(); 483 ``` 484 485 ### ExcelAnalysisWorker 486 487 Background plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker for analyzing Excel files. 488 489 #### Constructor 490 491 ```java 492 public ExcelAnalysisWorker( 493 Path filePath, 494 JProgressBar progressBar, 495 JButton startBtn, 496 ExcelAnalysisCallback callback 497 ) 498 ``` 499 500 ### ExcelSplitWorker 501 502 Background plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker for splitting Excel files. 503 504 #### Constructor 505 506 ```java 507 public ExcelSplitWorker( 508 Path outputPath, 509 Path orgFilePath, 510 JProgressBar progressBar, 511 JButton button, 512 Component parentComponent 513 ) 514 ``` 515 516 ### EmailSentWorker 517 518 Background plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker for sending emails. 519 520 #### Constructor 521 522 ```java 523 public EmailSentWorker( 524 String subject, 525 String body, 526 String taskId, 527 boolean isMassSent, 528 JProgressBar progressBar 529 ) 530 ``` 531 532 --- 533 534 ## Utility Classes 535 536 ### AppInfo 537 538 Application version and name constants. 539 540 **Location**: `src/main/java/fan/summer/utils/AppInfo.java` 541 542 ```java 543 public abstract class AppInfo { 544 public static final String VERSION = resolveVersion(); 545 public static final String NAME = loadProperty("app.name", "SwissKit"); 546 547 public static String getVersion(); 548 public static String getName(); 549 public static String getFullName(); // NAME + "-" + VERSION 550 } 551 ``` 552 553 ### EmailUtil 554 555 Email sending utility using Simple Java Mail library. Automatically loads SMTP configuration from database. 556 557 **Location**: `src/main/java/fan/summer/utils/EmailUtil.java` 558 559 ```java 560 public class EmailUtil { 561 /** 562 * Sends a plain text email. 563 */ 564 public static void sendText(String to, String subject, String body); 565 566 /** 567 * Tests SMTP connection using current configuration. 568 */ 569 public static boolean testConnection(); 570 571 /** 572 * Sends an email with full configuration. 573 */ 574 public static void sendEmail(EmailMessage message); 575 } 576 ``` 577 578 **Example Usage**: 579 580 ```java 581 // Plain text email 582 EmailUtil.sendText("to@example.com", "Subject", "Hello!"); 583 584 // HTML email with attachments 585 EmailUtil.sendEmail( 586 EmailMessage.builder() 587 .to("a@example.com", "b@example.com") 588 .cc("c@example.com") 589 .subject("Monthly Report") 590 .htmlBody("

Report

") 591 .attachments(new File("report.pdf")) 592 .build() 593 ); 594 595 // Test connection 596 if (EmailUtil.testConnection()) { 597 System.out.println("SMTP connection successful!"); 598 } 599 ``` 600 601 ### StringUtil 602 603 String validation utilities. 604 605 **Location**: `src/main/java/fan/summer/utils/StringUtil.java` 606 607 ```java 608 public class StringUtil { 609 /** 610 * Validates email address format. 611 */ 612 public static boolean checkEmail(String email); 613 } 614 ``` 615 616 ### UIUtils 617 618 Common UI component creation methods. 619 620 **Location**: `src/main/java/fan/summer/utils/UIUtils.java` 621 622 ```java 623 public class UIUtils { 624 // Color constants 625 public static final Color PRIMARY_COLOR = new Color(0xBB, 0x86, 0xFC); 626 public static final Color TEXT_COLOR = new Color(0x60, 0x60, 0x60); 627 public static final Color LIGHT_GRAY = new Color(0xF3, 0xF3, 0xF3); 628 629 // Factory methods 630 public static JPanel createSectionPanel(String title, JComponent content); 631 public static JLabel createPageTitle(String title); 632 public static JProgressBar createProgressBar(); 633 public static JPanel createCenterButtonPanel(JButton... buttons); 634 } 635 ``` 636 637 --- 638 639 ## Listeners 640 641 ### HeaderListener 642 643 Listener for extracting Excel headers. 644 645 **Location**: `src/main/java/fan/summer/kitpage/excel/listener/HeaderListener.java` 646 647 ```java 648 public class HeaderListener extends AnalysisEventListener> { 649 private Map headers = new HashMap<>(); 650 651 @Override 652 public void invokeHeadMap(Map headMap, AnalysisContext context); 653 654 @Override 655 public void invoke(Map data, AnalysisContext context); 656 657 public Map getHeaders(); 658 public void clear(); 659 } 660 ``` 661 662 ### NoModelDataListener 663 664 Listener for streaming Excel data. 665 666 **Location**: `src/main/java/fan/summer/kitpage/excel/listener/NoModelDataListener.java` 667 668 ```java 669 @Slf4j 670 public class NoModelDataListener extends AnalysisEventListener> { 671 private List> cachedDataList; 672 673 @Override 674 public void invoke(Map data, AnalysisContext context); 675 676 @Override 677 public void doAfterAllAnalysed(AnalysisContext context); 678 679 public List> getCachedDataList(); 680 public void clear(); 681 } 682 ``` 683 684 --- 685 686 ## Callback Interfaces 687 688 ### QueryAllEmailInfoCallBack 689 690 Callback interface for email info queries. 691 692 **Location**: 693 `src/main/java/fan/summer/kitpage/setting/plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker/second/QueryAllEmailInfoCallBack.java` 694 695 ```java 696 public interface QueryAllEmailInfoCallBack { 697 void onSuccess(List emailAddressBookEntities); 698 void onFailure(Exception e); 699 } 700 ``` 701 702 ### ExcelAnalysisCallback 703 704 Callback interface for Excel analysis results. 705 706 **Location**: `src/main/java/fan/summer/kitpage/excel/plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker/ExcelAnalysisCallback.java` 707 708 ```java 709 public interface ExcelAnalysisCallback { 710 void onSuccess(Map> result); 711 void onFailure(Exception e); 712 } 713 ``` 714 715 --- 716 717 **Need more help?** Check out the [Development Guide](development.md) for implementation examples!

1	package fan.summer.api;
2	
3	import javafx.scene.Node;
4	
5	/**
6	 * Interface implemented by all tools, both built-in and external plugins.
7	 * The host application depends solely on this interface, fully decoupled from implementations.
8	 *
9	 * Plugin developer steps:
10	 *   1. Declare SwissKitJ-Api as a provided dependency in pom.xml:
11	 *      {@code
12	 *      <dependency>
13	 *          <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
14	 *          <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
15	 *          <version>3.0.0</version>
16	 *          <scope>provided</scope>
17	 *      </dependency>
18	 *      }
19	 *   2. Implement this interface
20	 *   3. Declare the implementation's fully-qualified class name in
21	 *      META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin
22	 *   4. Package the plugin as a fat-JAR (include all private dependencies)
23	 *      and drop it into the host application's plugins/ directory
24	 */
25	public interface SwissKitJPlugin {
26	
27	    // ── Metadata (used by sidebar, search, and detail panel) ────────────
28	
29	    /** Globally unique ID; reverse-domain notation recommended: com.example.my-tool */
30	    String getId();
31	
32	    /** Display name shown on the tool card, e.g. "JSON Formatter" */
33	    String getName();
34	
35	    /** One-line description shown on the card and detail panel */
36	    String getDescription();
37	
38	    /** Category matching sidebar navigation. See {@link ToolCategory}. */
39	    ToolCategory getCategory();
40	
41	    /** Version string, e.g. "1.0.0" */
42	    String getVersion();
43	
44	    /**
45	     * Material Design Icons class name (without "mdi" prefix), e.g. "file-excel".
46	     * Full list: https://pictogrammers.com/library/mdi/
47	     * Override getIconNode() if a custom icon Node is needed.
48	     */
49	    String getMdiIcon();
50	
51	    /**
52	     * CSS class and colour for the icon background. See {@link IconStyle}.
53	     */
54	    default IconStyle getIconStyle() { return IconStyle.BLUE; }
55	
56	    /**
57	     * Type tag: built-in tools return {@link ToolType#BUILTIN},
58	     * external plugins return {@link ToolType#PLUGIN} (default).
59	     */
60	    default ToolType getType() { return ToolType.PLUGIN; }
61	
62	    // ── UI lifecycle ──────────────────────────────────────
63	
64	    /**
65	     * Returns the main UI node for this tool.
66	     * The host embeds it inside the content area StackPane.
67	     * Created once on first call; the same instance is reused thereafter.
68	     */
69	    Node createView();
70	
71	    /**
72	     * Called when the tool is brought to the foreground.
73	     * Use this to resume state, start timers, etc.
74	     * Default is a no-op; override as needed.
75	     */
76	    default void onActivate() {}
77	
78	    /**
79	     * Called when the tool is moved to the background.
80	     * Use this to pause timers, persist temporary state, etc.
81	     * Default is a no-op; override as needed.
82	     */
83	    default void onDeactivate() {}
84	
85	    /**
86	     * Called before the plugin is unloaded.
87	     * Release all resources here: threads, file handles, etc.
88	     * Default is a no-op; override as needed.
89	     */
90	    default void onUnload() {}
91	}
92

content.text:
The file has been rewritten. Here is a summary of all changes made to `docs/api.md`:

**Removed entirely:**
- `KitPage` interface (Swing era `JPanel`-based)
- `@SwissKitPage` annotation
- `SwissKitPageScanner` (SPI scanning is now done by `PluginLoader`)
- `GradientProgressBar`, `FixedWidthComboBox`, `SideMenuBar`, `TableUtil` (deleted Swing components)
- All `JPanel`, `JTable`, `SwingWorker`, `JProgressBar`, `JButton`, `Component` references

**Added:**
- `SwissKitJPlugin` interface -- full method table, code examples for both built-in tools and external plugins, SPI service descriptor instructions, registration flow explanation
- `ToolCategory` enum (DEV, TEXT, IMAGE, NET, OTHER)
- `ToolType` enum (BUILTIN, PLUGIN)
- `IconStyle` enum (BLUE, PURPLE, TEAL, AMBER, RED, PINK, GRAY) with CSS class and colour mappings
